methods.description: Process for collecting, processing, and analyzing Quercus pollen data using a volumetric spore trap, including different counting methods, data splicing, season definition, missing data handling, and calculation of various pollen season characteristics.

steps.description: Quercus pollen data (1996)(1997)(1998)(1999)(2000)(2001)(2002)(2003)(2004)(2005)(2006)(2007)(2008)(2009)(2010)(2011) were collected in Poznań by volumetric spore trap of the Hirst design (Hirst 1952).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect Quercus pollen data using Hirst volumetric spore trap.

steps.description: From 1996 to 1999, pollen data were collected following the methods outlined by Stach (2000) where pollen grains were counted along twelve latitudinal transects.
steps.searchable_summary: Count pollen grains using Stach method (1996-1999).

steps.description: From 2000 to 2011, this method was changed and pollen grains were counted along four longitudinal transects, which were divided into 2-mm (1 hourly) intervals, following the method described by the Spanish Aerobiological Network (Galán et al. 2007).
steps.searchable_summary: Count pollen grains using Spanish Aerobiological Network method (2000-2011).

steps.description: The two Quercus pollen datasets from Poznań were spliced together to make a single dataset running from 1996 to 2011.
steps.searchable_summary: Combine pollen datasets from different counting methods.

steps.description: Daily average and bi-hourly concentrations of pollen grains are expressed as P m -3 (Comtois 1998).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate and express pollen concentrations in P m^-3.

steps.description: The limits of the oak pollen season were calculated by using the 95 % method (Nilsson and Persson 1981) whereby the season starts when 2.5 % of the total catch was achieved and ends when 97.5 % is reached.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate pollen season start and end dates using the 95% method.

steps.description: Data related to start, end and peak day were converted to the day of the year from 1 January (DOY).
steps.searchable_summary: Convert dates (start, end, peak) to Day of Year (DOY).

steps.description: Due to the trap failure during the 2008 oak pollen season, the dataset contained missing values, which were replaced by the mean daily average Quercus pollen count for that day (1996-2001 mean): 3 days, 9-11 May 2008.
steps.searchable_summary: Impute missing pollen data using historical mean.
inputs: daily average pollen concentration (P m^-3) with missing values
outputs: daily average pollen concentration (P m^-3) with imputed values

steps.description: The following characteristics of the oak pollen season were examined: start date, end date, duration (number of days), the duration of the pre-peak period (number of days from the start of the oak pollen season to the peak date), the intensity of the oak pollen season (season pollen index, SPI), the timing and magnitude of the peak day (the highest daily average pollen concentration during pollen season), percentage of the total SPI recorded during 4 9 1 weekly periods during the oak pollen season (%) and the number of days during the season with daily average oak pollen levels [80 P m -3 .

methods.description: Process for obtaining and analyzing meteorological data (temperature, rainfall, RH) from a nearby station to assess their influence on oak pollen seasons.

steps.description: Meteorological data, i.e., daily average maximum, minimum and mean temperature, daily rainfall and relative humidity (RH), were obtained from the station located at Ławica Airport (52°25 0 N 16°49 0 E).
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain daily meteorological data (temperature, rainfall, RH).

steps.description: Fortnightly, monthly, 1.5-monthly and 2-monthly mean, maximum and minimum daily average temperatures and daily rainfall from the year preceding pollination (1995)(1996)(1997)(1998)(1999)(2000)(2001)(2002)(2003)(2004)(2005)(2006)(2007)(2008)(2009)(2010) were examined.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate aggregated meteorological parameters for periods in the preceding year.
inputs: daily meteorological data (temperature, rainfall) for preceding years (1995-2010)

steps.description: Fortnightly, monthly, 1.5-monthly and 2-monthly mean, maximum and minimum daily average temperatures and daily rainfall from the same year as pollination (1996-2011, January-April) were examined.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate aggregated meteorological parameters for periods in the current year (Jan-Apr).
inputs: daily meteorological data (temperature, rainfall) for current years (1996-2011, Jan-Apr)

steps.description: Averages of daily mean, maximum and minimum temperatures and daily rainfall recorded during the first and second week of oak pollen season and during the whole pollen season were examined.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate average meteorological parameters during specific pollen season periods.
outputs: average temp (mean, max, min) during 1st week of season
average rainfall during 1st week of season
average temp (mean, max, min) during 2nd week of season
average rainfall during 2nd week of season
average temp (mean, max, min) during whole season
